sections.paragraph:
m. (once f. Ecc 10:15)-

(1) heavy, wearisome labour, Ecc 1:3 2:11 used figuratively of the mind, Psa 73:16.

(2) the produce of labour, Psa 105:44 Ecc 2:19.

(3) weariness, trouble, vexation, Gr. κάματος, πόνος, Gen 41:51 Deu 26:7 Job 3:10 16:2 , מְנַחֲמֶי עָמָל “troublesome comforters.” Isa 53:11, מֵעֲמַל נַפְשֹׁו “of the sorrow (or anguish) of his soul.” It is rendered by some, sin, wickedness (i.q. אָוֶן), Num 23:21 Isa 10:1 but the signification of vexation is not unsuitable in both places.

(4) [Amal], pr.n. m. 1Ch 7:35.
